title: Mitochondrial Energy
definition: The role of mitochondria in generating ATP, the primary energy currency of cells, through oxidative phosphorylation.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Mitochondria are like the power plants within our cells, producing the energy we need to function.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Mitochondria generate ATP through oxidative phosphorylation, a process that relies on a specialized double membrane structure and an electron transport chain.
difficulty_levels.expert: The chemiosmotic theory explains the energy-generating mechanisms of mitochondria, providing insights into metabolic disorders, aging, and exercise physiology.

extra_fields.insight: Mitochondria, once free-living organisms, now serve as our cells' lifeblood, powering every aspect of our biology.
